name: Lee's Bakery
tagline: Chinatown's Vietnamese bakery with the best banh mi on the island and fresh-baked French bread daily.
mustOrder: Banh mi — classic Vietnamese sandwich on crispy French bread with pate and pickled veg
Pork hash — steamed dim sum-style dumpling, a local favorite
Coconut pandan cake — soft, fragrant, and not too sweet
parking: Street metered parking on River St. Chinatown garage nearby. Come early — they sell out.
talkStory: Lee's has been anchoring this corner of Chinatown forever. The banh mi is under $5 and it's better than most $15 sandwiches anywhere else. They bake the French bread fresh daily — you can smell it from the street. Get there before noon because popular items sell out. It's a takeout counter, not a sit-down spot — grab your banh mi and eat it at one of the Chinatown benches or take it to go. Cash is king here.
